// SETUP: This is a template. Copy or merge into your .claude/settings.json.
Replace every [FILL-IN] placeholder with project-specific content.
Hooks fire automatically — no manual invocation needed per turn.
Six hook entries total across 4 hook types; customize content per stack.
The additionalContext string is injected into Claude's context before
it responds — use it to reinforce rules that drift in long sessions.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[HOOK after Edit/Write] PRE-COMMIT CHECKLIST: (a) [FILL-IN: test command — e.g. npm test -- --run] ALL PASS (b) [FILL-IN: build command — e.g. npm run build] clean — catches Edit silent-fail that tests miss (c) run area audit skill matching files touched — use /skill-relevant to choose (d) GREP-PAIR: every router case has a matching handler def, count must match (e) trace at least one real caller for mutation paths. [EDIT-TOOL SILENT-FAIL TRAP] A parameter typo causes the Edit tool to error silently — always grep the expected text to confirm it landed. See .claude/rules/02-workflow.md for full checklist."}'

PostToolUse.// WHY: V6 anti-example: agent called Edit with a param typo, function body never landed, claimed committed. Runtime crash in production. This hook injects the pre-commit checklist after every file write so it cannot be skipped.

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "git[[:space:]]+commit"; then if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(fix|bug|bugfix)[:(]"; then echo "{\"additionalContext\": \"[D-CYCLE — fix commit detected] Rule D requires 4 artifacts per bug fix, NOT JUST the fix: (1) the code change (done), (2) adversarial test covering the failure class + 3+ nearby edges, (3) new V-entry in .claude/rules/00-session-start.md with YYYY-MM-DD + commit SHA + root cause, (4) new or extended audit-skill invariant to catch recurrence. Missing any of (2-4) = Rule D broken = same class of bug will ship again. If this commit lacks (2-4), plan a follow-up commit BEFORE next feature. Run /audit-health H1 to verify velocity.\"}"; elif echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(feat|feature)[:(]"; then echo "{\"additionalContext\": \"[D-CYCLE — feat commit detected] New features often duplicate existing helpers (Rule of 3) or create orphan collections (no reader). Pre-push checks: (a) grep shared modules first — did you check before writing? (b) any new collection/table has a reader AND writer AND retention policy (C3 lean schema), (c) Triangle Rule applied if replicating external behavior (capture real reference, never guess URL/method), (d) CLAUDE.md index updated if new public files added, (e) did you extract a helper for any pattern now ≥ 3 times (Rule of 3)? Run /audit-anti-vibe-code before push.\"}"; elif echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "refactor[:(]"; then echo "{\"additionalContext\": \"[D-CYCLE — refactor commit detected] Refactors are high-risk for subtle regressions because behavior should not change but often does. Verify: (a) /audit-rule-of-3 confirms the refactor actually consolidates (not just moves code), (b) full test suite passed, (c) no test needed to change — if tests DID change, refactor is not behavior-preserving and the scope changed mid-turn (update TodoList per rules/02-workflow.md \\\"Scope expansion\\\" section), (d) grep the refactored symbol to confirm all call sites updated.\"}"; elif echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "(perf|performance)[:(]"; then echo "{\"additionalContext\": \"[D-CYCLE — perf commit detected] Performance changes often regress correctness. Verify: (a) adversarial tests still pass (perf ≠ correctness), (b) benchmark numbers in commit body (before/after), (c) no caching introduced without invalidation strategy, (d) if React: /audit-react-patterns (memo, useCallback, key props).\"}"; fi; fi; exit 0'
hooks.// WHY: Rule D (continuous improvement) demands 4 artifacts per bug fix, not 1. Without the hook, agent ships 'fix + push' and forgets the test/V-entry/audit invariant half the time. The hook detects conventional-commit prefix on any git commit and injects the type-specific reminder so the V-log keeps pace with feature velocity. Supports fix, feat, refactor, perf.

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); CMD=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".tool_input.command // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "[FILL-IN: deploy command pattern — e.g. vercel --prod]"; then if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE "[FILL-IN: areas that are commit-only, never deployed — e.g. (backend|Backend|admin-only)]"; then echo "BLOCKED: [FILL-IN: reason message — e.g. Backend files are commit-only. Use git push only. vercel --prod = Vercel cost for files that run locally.]." >&2; exit 2; fi; fi; exit 0'
hooks.// WHY: Prevents deploying file areas that should only be committed (e.g. backend-only tabs, dev-only scaffolding, admin-only dashboards). Two FILL-IN patterns: first detects deploy command, second detects the blocked area.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[MANDATORY FIRST READ: .claude/rules/00-session-start.md — character + expectations + iron-clad rules A-H + past violations V1-Vn + pre-commit checklist. READ BEFORE ANY TOOL CALL.] [IRON-CLAD SUMMARY] A: bug → revert, never patch forward. B: [FILL-IN: deploy safety probe — e.g. probe unauth write paths before + after firestore rules deploy]. C: Rule of 3 / crypto.getRandomValues for tokens / no uid in public docs / no allow:if-true without comment. D: every bug → adversarial test + rule update + audit invariant — use /audit-health to verify velocity. E: [FILL-IN: data-boundary rule — e.g. backend UI reads our DB only]. F: Triangle Rule (UNIVERSAL form — docs/triangle-rule.md) — Evidence + Intention + Existing code before writing; never guess URL/method/field. G: load deferred tools/skills as needed — rules still apply. G.2: same manual pattern ≥ 3 times → promote to skill (/capability-scout). H: [FILL-IN: data-ownership rule]. [DEPLOY] Requires explicit THIS-TURN authorization — prior auth does not roll over to next commit or next turn. [GROWTH ENGINES] The template compounds via 4 engines — docs/growth-engine.md — skip any one and the loop breaks."}'

SessionStart.// WHY: Without a SessionStart hook, the agent starts cold with no awareness of the iron-clad rules. The hook injects a mandatory-read reminder even if the user forgets to invoke /session-start manually.

hooks.command: echo '{"additionalContext": "[IRON-CLAD PRE-PROMPT — every turn] A: bug → revert. B: [FILL-IN: deploy safety short form]. C: Rule of 3 / crypto tokens / lean schema / no uid leaks. D: bug → test + rule + audit invariant. E: [FILL-IN: data boundary short form]. F: Triangle — Evidence + Intention + Existing code before guessing URL/method/field. G: load tools/skills on demand (no ask). G.2: rep ≥ 3 → promote. Deploy = new explicit ask every turn. Full rules: .claude/rules/00-session-start.md. Health dashboard: /audit-health."}'

UserPromptSubmit.// WHY: Iron-clad rules drift in long conversations. Injecting a summary every prompt keeps the rules in active context without the agent having to re-read the rule files each turn. Pair with rules/00-session-start.md for the full text.

hooks.command: bash -c 'INPUT=$(cat); PROMPT=$(echo "$INPUT" | jq -r ".prompt // empty" 2>/dev/null || echo ""); RISKS=""; echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE "(delete|drop table|truncate|rm -rf|reset --hard|force.push|force-push)" && RISKS="$RISKS destructive"; echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE "(deploy|vercel --prod|firebase deploy|kubectl apply|production|prod)" && RISKS="$RISKS deploy"; echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE "(migrate|migration|schema change|new collection|new table)" && RISKS="$RISKS schema"; echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE "(secret|api.key|credential|password|token)" && RISKS="$RISKS secret"; echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE "(rewrite|refactor all|clean up everything|simplify rules)" && RISKS="$RISKS mass-rewrite"; if [ -n "$RISKS" ]; then echo "{\"additionalContext\": \"[RISK DETECTED in user prompt:$RISKS] Before acting: DESTRUCTIVE → confirm with user; prefer non-destructive alternative (git revert over force-push; soft delete over DROP). DEPLOY → require explicit THIS-TURN auth; prior auth never rolls over; run Probe-Deploy-Probe if infra rules touched (rule B). SCHEMA → migration plan MUST include fallback removal upfront (methodology AP5); no orphan collection (C3 — reader+writer+retention). SECRET → env vars only; never inline in src/ or api/; rotate immediately if leaked to commit. MASS-REWRITE → Rule-Compression anti-pattern (methodology AP4); never summarize rules — split/expand/reorganize only. Re-read .claude/rules/01-iron-clad.md before acting.\"}"; fi; exit 0'
hooks.// WHY: User prompts containing high-risk keywords (delete, deploy, drop, secret, rewrite) trigger an extra-attention reminder so the agent surfaces the relevant iron-clad rule and anti-pattern before executing. Pair with the always-on iron-clad summary hook above.
